description_long: This object is a small crustacean, likely a crab, positioned on a textured sandy surface. Its body is compact and rounded, with a hard exoskeleton that appears mottled in shades of brown and gray. The crustacean has multiple slender legs radiating outward, some of which are slightly curved and splayed, giving it a spindly appearance. Two prominent claws are visible at the front, slightly raised and distinct from the walking legs. The carapace is slightly domed, and subtle segmentation is visible between the cephalothorax and the abdomen. The overall surface of the crustacean is rough and matte, with a natural, weathered look typical of marine life washed ashore.
description: A small, brown-gray crab with a hard, rough shell and multiple spindly legs, resting on a sandy surface. Its claws and segmented body are clearly visible.
description_view_0: Front view showing the crab's claws extended forward and the rounded carapace with legs spread out.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the length and curvature of the legs, with the body appearing flatter against the sand.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the carapace and the arrangement of the legs trailing behind.
description_view_3: Angled view showing both claws and the segmentation between the cephalothorax and abdomen.
